sast.title: User-controlled data is injected into HTTP response headers without newline sanitization. An attacker can inject CRLF characters to split the HTTP response, potentially leading to cache poisoning, XSS, or session fixation. Sanitize all user input by removing or encoding CR (\\r) and LF (\\n) characters before setting response headers.
sast.description: User-controlled data is injected into HTTP response headers without newline sanitization. An attacker can inject CRLF characters to split the HTTP response, potentially leading to cache poisoning, XSS, or session fixation. Sanitize all user input by removing or encoding CR (\\r) and LF (\\n) characters before setting response headers.
cwe: CWE-113: Improper Neutralization of CRLF Sequences in HTTP Headers

sast_false_positives.title: sqlalchemy.text passes the constructed SQL statement to the database mostly unchanged. This means that the usual SQL injection protections are not applied and this function is vulnerable to SQL injection if user input can reach here. Use normal SQLAlchemy operators (such as `or_()`, `and_()`, etc.) to construct SQL.
